helpful: Provisioned petition text made the hold-for-Jules/GVR posture obvious without any PDF fetching.
CourtListener MCP opinions endpoint delivered the full Jules slip opinion — the single decisive input for this cell.
fedcourts query --disposition gvr --era 2020s gave a concrete recent-GVR reference class (dates, distribution counts).
gaps: The committed statpack has no cut for call-for-response (CFR) petitions; a CFR-conditional grant/GVR base rate would have sharpened this estimate more than the relist/CVSG cuts.
The statpack.md in the repo lacks the per-Term salience-band section the prompt describes, so the band-anchoring step could not be applied.
No cheap way to pull only a SCOTUS opinion's syllabus/holding via MCP — the clusters syllabus field was empty and search exposes no snippet, so the full slip opinion had to be fetched.
notes: Forward cell rode an intervening decision (Jules, 2026-05-14) rather than base rates; corpus GVR priors were used to sanity-check the disposition label and timing pattern.
